[[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (Deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okazaki]] [[Category:Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}} [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]]

This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (Deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okazaki]] [[Category:Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}} [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]]

{{Shrine | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:八柱神社 鳥居（夏）.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]] 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E|region:JP-23_scale:5000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yahashira Shrine}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)
[[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okazaki]] [[Category:Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}} [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]]

{{神社 | 名称 = 八柱神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:八柱神社 鳥居（夏）.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[欠町]]石ヶ崎52-1 | 位置 = {{Coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E|region:JP-23_scale:5000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=八柱神社}} | 祭神 = [[熊野権現]]（五男三女神）
[[天照大神|大日霊女命]]
[[大国主|大己貴命]]
若宮大権現
[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]
築山神明宮 | 創建 = [[天正]]7年{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | 社格 = [[近代社格制度|村社]] | 例祭 = [[例祭|例大祭]]、[[歳旦祭|元旦祭]]、[[月次祭]]、[[大晦日]] | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=八柱神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''八柱神社'''（やはしらじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[欠町]]にある[[神社]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ。[[天正]]7年創建{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}。 == 社号の由来 == [[神道]]において神様の[[助数詞|数え方]]は「柱」を用いる。[[熊野権現]]の5男神と3[[女神]]の8神を主祭神とし祀っていることから'''「八柱神社」'''と名付けられた。 ==旧社号と八柱神社の成り立ち== '''旧社号'''
旧社号を'''「八王子大権現」'''という{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}。 社号の変更は、[[1868年|明治元年]]に[[明治]]新政府から発布された[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]が関連している。令の発布により、[[岡崎藩]]においても社号改称のお触れが出された。一例によると「八王子」については「八柱社」に変更。[[本地垂迹]]の思想が色濃い「大[[権現]]」の記載は削除され、現在の社号の礎になったとみられる{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}。 '''成り立ち'''
旧欠村にある廣見に祀られていた大日女靈尊（[[天照大神]]）と大己貴命（[[大国主|大国主命]]）、また[[明和]]4(1767)年に勧請された御鍬大神社の'''3柱を祀っていた社'''。
現）八柱神社がある石ケ崎には、[[松平信康|岡崎信康]]が[[岡崎城]]主だった際の岡崎町奉行のひとり、[[松平新右衛門]]の[[義兄弟姉妹|義兄]]、且つ、元は[[紀伊国|紀伊]]の生まれで桜井村([[安城市]][[桜井町 (安城市)|桜井町]]付近）より欠村に越してきた鈴木墨右衛門重辰が勧請した'''（熊野）権現社'''。[[大永]]2(1522)年から[[天正]]17(1589)年の間、欠の郷の[[領主]]であった柴田一族の六郎が出家し開基・上意により神明宮[[別当]]となった[[裕傳寺]]{{Sfn|『参河聰視禄　欠村記』}}{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第参巻』|p=306}}より、[[正保]]3([[1646年|1646]])年に欠山に改葬された[[築山殿]]の御霊を祀る'''神明宮'''から成る。
これらの社と神明宮を総称し'''「八王子大権現」'''と号したとされる{{Sfn|『参河聰視禄　欠村記』}}。 '''名勝として'''
[[豊橋市|豊橋]][[上伝馬町|上伝馬]]の金物商であった夏目可敬が[[嘉永]]4([[1851年|1851]])年から8年を掛けて[[三河国|三河]]の[[名勝]]を記した『参河國名所圖繪』には欠村の名勝として、権現社と神明宮が記載されている{{Sfn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}。
また、[[寛政]]年間に[[江戸幕府]]が[[道中奉行]]に命じ文化3(1806)年に完成した『東海道分間延絵図』 [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/ 東京国立博物館]に於いても「鈴木墨右衛門屋敷跡」という記載と共に現在の八柱神社の所在地付近に[[鳥居]]の印が描かれている。 [https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/yokohama/tokaido/02_tokaido/04_qa/index6/answer2.htm 国土交通省 関東地方整備局 横浜国道事務所HP] 『岡崎市史　第七巻』によると現八柱神社に座していた権現社・神明宮は[[大正]]2年2月22日に廣見に一度[[遷宮|遷座]]されたという記載がある。{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}} '''度々改編される由緒'''
原本が[[内閣文庫]]に保存されている、[[幕末|江戸時代末期]]に加茂久算が記した[[地誌]]『参河聰視禄　欠村記』においてはそれぞれの[[祭神]]・[[例祭]]・[[寺社領|社地]]/[[境内]]広さ・社/[[神社建築|拝殿]]寸法などが事細かく記されている{{Sfn|『参河聰視禄　欠村記』}}。
その一方で、[[大正]]15(1926)年から[[昭和]]10(1935)年に[[岡崎市役所]]から刊行された『岡崎市史　第七巻』においては大己貴命・権現社・築山殿御霊は削除され、以下のように記されている{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}。加えて、『岡崎市史』の編さん人を務めた[[伊賀八幡宮]]の宮司の嫡男・[[柴田顕正]]は以下引用文の内容を補填するために『参河聰視禄』を参照として用いている{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}。 {{SquoteH}}大正二年二月二十二日同町石ケ崎より無格社神明社を合祀した、此神明社の祭神を、今は天照大神となせど、實は徳川家康の室築山殿の靈を祀つたものであると傳へて居る。祭神は、五男三女の神、天照大神である。然るに参河総視禄には、石ケ崎神明社を合祀せざる以前、祭神二座、大日女靈尊、大己貴命となせるが、何に據つたものであろう。（参考の條参照）{{SquoteF}} {{Efn|幅広い関連文献や岡崎市内各村より提供された資料に基づき[[柴田顕正]]が『岡崎市史』を編さんしていることがリンク先にて確認できる}} 近年では、創建年度不明（神社内由緒）、及び鎌倉時代後期創建（神社内配布チラシ）と由緒の記載に乖離が見られる。
[[File:八柱神社 説明.jpg|thumb|八柱神社 説明]] '''近年伝わる由緒'''
古来、[[熊野]]に居住していた[[清和源氏]]の流れをくむ鈴木一族が[[治承・寿永の乱|源平合戦]]を経て、しばらく後に碧海郡桜井村（現在の[[安城市]][[桜井町 (愛知県)|桜井町]]）に移り住んだ。その後、一族の一部が[[三河国]]（現在の[[岡崎市]]）欠村（現在の[[欠町]]、[[元欠町]]、[[根石町]]、栄町、朝日町4丁目、若宮町3丁目{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=643}}、及び西欠町）に再度移住。日ごろより崇敬していた[[熊野権現]]を同村・広見の森中に奉斎する。その[[奥宮]]を同村北方の山頂（現在地）に祀った。以降、鈴木一族の[[氏神]]として尊崇されたが欠村の発展と近隣住民の増加等、時代の移り変わりを経て同村の[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められるようになった。 現在は近隣地域の[[鎮守神|鎮守]]とし崇敬されている。 === 歴史 === *[[天正]]7年(1579年） - 創建{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} *[[正保]]3年(1646年） - [[築山殿]]の首塚の改葬{{Sfn|『『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[明和]]4年（1767年） - 近隣に在った農業の[[守り神]]、御鍬神社を[[合祀]] * [[明治]]5年（1872年） - [[近代社格制度|村社]]に列する * 明治42年（1909年） - [[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定 * [[大正]]2年（1913年） - 築山神明宮を本殿下陣に合祀 * [[昭和]]9年（1934年） - [[欠町]]広見西通りから現在地に移転[[遷宮]] * 昭和52年（1977年） - [[築山殿|築山御前]]の[[首塚]]の改築 === 祭神 === * [[熊野権現]]（五男三女神） : [[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] : [[アメノホヒ|天菩卑命]] : [[アマツヒコネ|天日子根命]] : [[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] : [[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] : [[タキリビメ|多紀理毘賣命]] : [[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比賣命]] : [[タギツヒメ|多岐都比賣命]] * [[天照大神|大日霊女命]]（天照大神） * [[大国主|大己貴命]]（大国主命） * 若宮大権現（[[松平信康]]公の御霊） * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]（豊穣の神） * 築山神明宮（[[築山殿|築山御前]]の御霊） === 合祀 === [[ファイル:築山御前首塚１.jpg|サムネイル|築山御前首塚]] [[天正]]7年（1579年）、[[織田信長]]の命により小藪村（現在の[[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]冨塚）で殺害された[[築山殿|築山御前]]の御霊を祀る。[[築山殿|築山御前]]の首は、[[織田信長]]の[[実検]]を受けた後に[[真宗高田派]]の祐傳寺（[[岡崎市]][[両町 (岡崎市)|両町]]）に[[埋葬]]されたが[[正保]]3年(1646年)に八柱神社の境内に[[改葬]]された。 '''改葬の経緯''' [[正保]]2年([[1645年]]）に[[岡崎藩|岡崎藩主]]となった[[水野忠善]]は、[[田中吉政]]より始まり、その後の岡崎藩主・[[本多氏|本多家]]へと引き継がれた城下町整備を引き続き行った。 正保3年（[[1646年]]）に水野忠善は投町（現・[[若宮町 (岡崎市)|若宮町]]）南に位置し、[[築山殿]]の首塚があった祐傳寺を[[両町 (岡崎市)|両町]]に移転させ、その跡地に下級武士の組屋敷を拡張したとされる。この際、欠村以西が岡崎城下となっている{{Sfn|『『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}}。 === 例祭日 === * [[例祭|例大祭]] - 10月18日 * [[歳旦祭|元旦祭]] - 1月2日 * [[月次祭]] - 9月を除き、毎月18日（9月は10日に執り行う。[[二百二十日|二百二十日祭]]） === ロケ地として === [[岡崎市]]が映像制作の[[ロケーション撮影]]を誘致していることにより [https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m 岡崎フィルムコミッション] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト　岡崎フィルムコミッション、八柱神社も映像のロケ地として活用されている[https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ。 '''作品名''' * 映画『朽ちないサクラ』 [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie 朽ちないサクラ] - 映画『朽ちないサクラ』製作委員会 ―2024年6月21日公開 == 注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)}} * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ * [https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/yokohama/tokaido/02_tokaido/04_qa/index6/answer2.htm 国土交通省 関東地方整備局 横浜国道事務所HP] - 国土交通省 関東地方整備局 横浜国道事務所 * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/ 東京国立博物館　研究情報アーカイブス] - 東京国立博物館 * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ *[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m 岡崎フィルムコミッション] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ *[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie 朽ちないサクラ] - 映画『朽ちないサクラ』製作委員会 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やはしらしんしや}} [[Category:岡崎市の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:八柱神社 鳥居（夏）.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]] 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E|region:JP-23_scale:5000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yahashira Shrine}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)
[[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okazaki]] [[Category:Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)-qq]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}} [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]]

Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okazaki]] [[Category:Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)-qq]] [[Category:Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine (Okazaki City)}} [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E6%9F%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]]

It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * {{Shrine | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:八柱神社 鳥居（夏）.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]] 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E|region:JP-23_scale:5000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yahashira Shrine}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)
[[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okazaki]] {{Shrine | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:八柱神社 鳥居（夏）.jpg|250px]] | location = [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki]], [[Aichi Prefecture]] 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E|region:JP-23_scale:5000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yahashira Shrine}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)
[[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined {{神社 | 名称 = 八柱神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:八柱神社 鳥居（夏）.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[欠町]]石ヶ崎52-1 | 位置 = {{Coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E|region:JP-23_scale:5000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=八柱神社}} | 祭神 = [[熊野権現]]（五男三女神）
[[天照大神|大日霊女命]]
[[大国主|大己貴命]]
若宮大権現
[[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]
築山神明宮 | 創建 = [[天正]]7年{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | 社格 = [[近代社格制度|村社]] | 例祭 = [[例祭|例大祭]]、[[歳旦祭|元旦祭]]、[[月次祭]]、[[大晦日]] | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=八柱神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''八柱神社'''（やはしらじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[欠町]]にある[[神社]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ。[[天正]]7年創建{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}。 == 社号の由来 == [[神道]]において神様の[[助数詞|数え方]]は「柱」を用いる。[[熊野権現]]の5男神と3[[女神]]の8神を主祭神とし祀っていることから'''「八柱神社」'''と名付けられた。 ==旧社号と八柱神社の成り立ち== '''旧社号'''
旧社号を'''「八王子大権現」'''という{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}。 社号の変更は、[[1868年|明治元年]]に[[明治]]新政府から発布された[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]が関連している。令の発布により、[[岡崎藩]]においても社号改称のお触れが出された。一例によると「八王子」については「八柱社」に変更。[[本地垂迹]]の思想が色濃い「大[[権現]]」の記載は削除され、現在の社号の礎になったとみられる{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}。 '''成り立ち'''
旧欠村にある廣見に祀られていた大日女靈尊（[[天照大神]]）と大己貴命（[[大国主|大国主命]]）、また[[明和]]4(1767)年に勧請された御鍬大神社の'''3柱を祀っていた社'''。
現）八柱神社がある石ケ崎には、[[松平信康|岡崎信康]]が[[岡崎城]]主だった際の岡崎町奉行のひとり、[[松平新右衛門]]の[[義兄弟姉妹|義兄]]、且つ、元は[[紀伊国|紀伊]]の生まれで桜井村([[安城市]][[桜井町 (安城市)|桜井町]]付近）より欠村に越してきた鈴木墨右衛門重辰が勧請した'''（熊野）権現社'''。[[大永]]2(1522)年から[[天正]]17(1589)年の間、欠の郷の[[領主]]であった柴田一族の六郎が出家し開基・上意により神明宮[[別当]]となった[[裕傳寺]]{{Sfn|『参河聰視禄　欠村記』}}{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第参巻』|p=306}}より、[[正保]]3([[1646年|1646]])年に欠山に改葬された[[築山殿]]の御霊を祀る'''神明宮'''から成る。
これらの社と神明宮を総称し'''「八王子大権現」'''と号したとされる{{Sfn|『参河聰視禄　欠村記』}}。 '''名勝として'''
[[豊橋市|豊橋]][[上伝馬町|上伝馬]]の金物商であった夏目可敬が[[嘉永]]4([[1851年|1851]])年から8年を掛けて[[三河国|三河]]の[[名勝]]を記した『参河國名所圖繪』には欠村の名勝として、権現社と神明宮が記載されている{{Sfn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}。
また、[[寛政]]年間に[[江戸幕府]]が[[道中奉行]]に命じ文化3(1806)年に完成した『東海道分間延絵図』 [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/imgsearch/ 東京国立博物館]に於いても「鈴木墨右衛門屋敷跡」という記載と共に現在の八柱神社の所在地付近に[[鳥居]]の印が描かれている。 [https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/yokohama/tokaido/02_tokaido/04_qa/index6/answer2.htm 国土交通省 関東地方整備局 横浜国道事務所HP] 『岡崎市史　第七巻』によると現八柱神社に座していた権現社・神明宮は[[大正]]2年2月22日に廣見に一度[[遷宮|遷座]]されたという記載がある。{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}} '''度々改編される由緒'''
原本が[[内閣文庫]]に保存されている、[[幕末|江戸時代末期]]に加茂久算が記した[[地誌]]『参河聰視禄　欠村記』においてはそれぞれの[[祭神]]・[[例祭]]・[[寺社領|社地]]/[[境内]]広さ・社/[[神社建築|拝殿]]寸法などが事細かく記されている{{Sfn|『参河聰視禄　欠村記』}}。
その一方で、[[大正]]15(1926)年から[[昭和]]10(1935)年に[[岡崎市役所]]から刊行された『岡崎市史　第七巻』においては大己貴命・権現社・築山殿御霊は削除され、以下のように記されている{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}。加えて、『岡崎市史』の編さん人を務めた[[伊賀八幡宮]]の宮司の嫡男・[[柴田顕正]]は以下引用文の内容を補填するために『参河聰視禄』を参照として用いている{{Sfn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}。 {{SquoteH}}大正二年二月二十二日同町石ケ崎より無格社神明社を合祀した、此神明社の祭神を、今は天照大神となせど、實は徳川家康の室築山殿の靈を祀つたものであると傳へて居る。祭神は、五男三女の神、天照大神である。然るに参河総視禄には、石ケ崎神明社を合祀せざる以前、祭神二座、大日女靈尊、大己貴命となせるが、何に據つたものであろう。（参考の條参照）{{SquoteF}} {{Efn|幅広い関連文献や岡崎市内各村より提供された資料に基づき[[柴田顕正]]が『岡崎市史』を編さんしていることがリンク先にて確認できる}} 近年では、創建年度不明（神社内由緒）、及び鎌倉時代後期創建（神社内配布チラシ）と由緒の記載に乖離が見られる。
[[File:八柱神社 説明.jpg|thumb|八柱神社 説明]] '''近年伝わる由緒'''
古来、[[熊野]]に居住していた[[清和源氏]]の流れをくむ鈴木一族が[[治承・寿永の乱|源平合戦]]を経て、しばらく後に碧海郡桜井村（現在の[[安城市]][[桜井町 (愛知県)|桜井町]]）に移り住んだ。その後、一族の一部が[[三河国]]（現在の[[岡崎市]]）欠村（現在の[[欠町]]、[[元欠町]]、[[根石町]]、栄町、朝日町4丁目、若宮町3丁目{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=643}}、及び西欠町）に再度移住。日ごろより崇敬していた[[熊野権現]]を同村・広見の森中に奉斎する。その[[奥宮]]を同村北方の山頂（現在地）に祀った。以降、鈴木一族の[[氏神]]として尊崇されたが欠村の発展と近隣住民の増加等、時代の移り変わりを経て同村の[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められるようになった。 現在は近隣地域の[[鎮守神|鎮守]]とし崇敬されている。 === 歴史 === *[[天正]]7年(1579年） - 創建{{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} *[[正保]]3年(1646年） - [[築山殿]]の首塚の改葬{{Sfn|『『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[明和]]4年（1767年） - 近隣に在った農業の[[守り神]]、御鍬神社を[[合祀]]する。 * [[明治]]5年（1872年） - [[近代社格制度|村社]]に列する。 * 明治42年（1909年） - [[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定される。 * [[大正]]2年（1913年） - 築山神明宮を本殿下陣に合祀される。 * [[昭和]]9年（1934年） - [[欠町]]広見西通りから現在地に移転[[遷宮]] * 昭和52年（1977年） - [[築山殿|築山御前]]の[[首塚]]の改築工事 === 祭神 === * [[熊野権現]]（五男三女神） : [[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] : [[アメノホヒ|天菩卑命]] : [[アマツヒコネ|天日子根命]] : [[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] : [[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] : [[タキリビメ|多紀理毘賣命]] : [[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比賣命]] : [[タギツヒメ|多岐都比賣命]] * [[天照大神|大日霊女命]]（天照大神） * [[大国主|大己貴命]]（大国主命） * 若宮大権現（[[松平信康]]公の御霊） * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受姫大神]]（豊穣の神） * 築山神明宮（[[築山殿|築山御前]]の御霊） === 合祀 === [[ファイル:築山御前首塚１.jpg|サムネイル|築山御前首塚]] [[天正]]7年（1579年）、[[織田信長]]の命により小藪村（現在の[[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]冨塚）で殺害された[[築山殿|築山御前]]の御霊を祀る。[[築山殿|築山御前]]の首は、[[織田信長]]の[[実検]]を受けた後に[[真宗高田派]]の祐傳寺（[[岡崎市]][[両町 (岡崎市)|両町]]）に[[埋葬]]されたが[[正保]]3年(1646年)に八柱神社の境内に[[改葬]]された。 '''改葬の経緯''' [[正保]]2年([[1645年]]）に[[岡崎藩|岡崎藩主]]となった[[水野忠善]]は、[[田中吉政]]より始まり、その後の岡崎藩主・[[本多氏|本多家]]へと引き継がれた城下町整備を引き続き行った。 正保3年（[[1646年]]）に水野忠善は投町（現・[[若宮町 (岡崎市)|若宮町]]）南に位置し、[[築山殿]]の首塚があった祐傳寺を[[両町 (岡崎市)|両町]]に移転させ、その跡地に下級武士の組屋敷を拡張したとされる。この際、欠村以西が岡崎城下となっている{{Sfn|『『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}}。 === 例祭日 === * [[例祭|例大祭]] - 10月18日 * [[歳旦祭|元旦祭]] - 1月2日 * [[月次祭]] - 9月を除き、毎月18日（9月は10日に執り行う。[[二百二十日|二百二十日祭]]） === ロケ地として === [[岡崎市]]が映像制作の[[ロケーション撮影]]を誘致していることにより [https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m 岡崎フィルムコミッション] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト　岡崎フィルムコミッション、八柱神社も映像のロケ地として活用されている[https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ。 '''作品名''' * 映画『朽ちないサクラ』 [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie 朽ちないサクラ] - 映画『朽ちないサクラ』製作委員会 ―2024年6月21日公開 == 注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)}} * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ * [https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/yokohama/tokaido/02_tokaido/04_qa/index6/answer2.htm 国土交通省 関東地方整備局 横浜国道事務所HP] - 国土交通省 関東地方整備局 横浜国道事務所 * [https://webarchives.tnm.jp/ 東京国立博物館　研究情報アーカイブス] - 東京国立博物館 * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 八柱神社] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ *[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m 岡崎フィルムコミッション] - 愛知県岡崎市公式観光サイト・岡崎おでかけナビ *[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie 朽ちないサクラ] - 映画『朽ちないサクラ』製作委員会 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やはしらしんしや}} [[Category:岡崎市の神社]]

Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reitaisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], [[Tsukinamisai]], [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[Okazaki City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[Meiji government]] in [[1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{citation needed|Yahashira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okazaki]] 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[Taishō]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): - [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] - [[Ame-no-Hohi]] - [[Amatsuhikone]] - [[Ikutsuhikone]] - [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] - [[Takiribime|Takirihime]] - [[Ichikishimahime]] - [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[Taishō]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s burial mound == Filming location == Due to [[Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
