[[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] in [[:en:1868|1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[:en:Okazaki Domain|Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[:en:Okazaki Castle|Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[:en:Toyohashi|Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q119324965]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:八柱神社 鳥居（春）.jpg | location = [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95390555555556|N|137.18745833333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)
[[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] in [[:en:1868|1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[:en:Okazaki Domain|Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[:en:Okazaki Castle|Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[:en:Toyohashi|Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q119324965]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:八柱神社 鳥居（春）.jpg | location = [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95390555555556|N|137.18745833333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)
[[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]]
[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]
Wakamiya Daigongen
[[Toyouke Bime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]]
Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] in [[:en:1868|1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[:en:Okazaki Domain|Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[:en:Okazaki Castle|Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[:en:Toyohashi|Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] in [[:en:1868|1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[:en:Okazaki Domain|Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[:en:Okazaki Castle|Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[:en:Toyohashi|Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)}} [[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q119324965}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] in [[:en:1868|1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[:en:Okazaki Domain|Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[:en:Okazaki Castle|Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[:en:Toyohashi|Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)}} [[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q119324965}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q119324965]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:八柱神社 鳥居（春）.jpg | location = [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95390555555556|N|137.18745833333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)

[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]

Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]] in [[:en:1868|1868]]. This decree led to shrine name changes within the [[:en:Okazaki Domain|Okazaki Domain]], such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to [[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]], a [[daimyo]] of [[:en:Okazaki Castle|Okazaki Castle]]. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from [[:en:Toyohashi|Toyohashi]], over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** [[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ([[:en:Matsudaira Nobuyasu|Matsudaira Nobuyasu]]) * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Hime no Mikoto]] (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ([[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 3 (1646): Reburial of [[:en:Lady Tsukiyama|Lady Tsukiyama]]’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)}} [[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q119324965}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ({{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}} (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō}} 3 (1646): Reburial of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)]][[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q119324965]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yahashira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:八柱神社 鳥居（春）.jpg | location = [[Kakemachi]], {{ill|Okazaki|en|Okazaki|WD=Q402810|lt=Okazaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} 52-1 Ishigasaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34.95390555555556|N|137.18745833333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female gods)

[[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]]

Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}[https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan}} in {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}}. This decree led to shrine name changes within the {{ill|Okazaki Domain|en|Okazaki Domain|WD=Q905686|lt=Okazaki Domain}}, such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to {{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}, a [[daimyo]] of {{ill|Okazaki Castle|en|Okazaki Castle|WD=Q1013445|lt=Okazaki Castle}}. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from {{ill|Toyohashi|en|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431|lt=Toyohashi}}, over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}} ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ({{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}} (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō}} 3 (1646): Reburial of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}[https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan}} in {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}}. This decree led to shrine name changes within the {{ill|Okazaki Domain|en|Okazaki Domain|WD=Q905686|lt=Okazaki Domain}}, such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to {{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}, a [[daimyo]] of {{ill|Okazaki Castle|en|Okazaki Castle|WD=Q1013445|lt=Okazaki Castle}}. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from {{ill|Toyohashi|en|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431|lt=Toyohashi}}, over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone}} ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ({{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto}} (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō}} 3 (1646): Reburial of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama}}’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)}} [[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q119324965}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860|1=Kake-machi, Okazaki}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}[https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} in {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}. This decree led to shrine name changes within the {{ill|Okazaki Domain|en|Okazaki Domain|WD=Q905686|lt=Okazaki Domain|1=Okazaki Domain}}, such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to {{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu|1=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}, a [[daimyo]] of {{ill|Okazaki Castle|en|Okazaki Castle|WD=Q1013445|lt=Okazaki Castle|1=Okazaki Castle}}. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama|1=Lady Tsukiyama}}{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from {{ill|Toyohashi|en|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431|lt=Toyohashi|1=Toyohashi}}, over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ({{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu|1=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama|1=Lady Tsukiyama}}) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō|1=Shōhō}} 3 (1646): Reburial of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama|1=Lady Tsukiyama}}’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine | consecration_year = [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{Sfn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} | Rank = [[Village Shrine]] (under the [[Modern Shrine System]]) | Festivals = [[Reisai|festival]], [[Saitansai|New Year's Festival]], {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[Ōmisoka]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=57|lat_sec=14.06|lon_deg=137|lon_min=11|lon_sec=14.85|label=Yahashira Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|14.06|N|137|11|14.85|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kake-machi, Okazaki|WD=Q17226860|1=Kake-machi, Okazaki}} }} '''Yahashira Shrine''' (八柱神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Kakemachi]], [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}[https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website. It was founded in [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579){{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}}. == Origin of the name == In [[Shinto]], the [[counters in Japanese|counter]] for deities is "pillar." As the main enshrined deities are the eight gods (five male and three female) of [[Kumano Gongen]], the shrine was named '''Yahashira Shrine''' ("Eight Pillars Shrine"). == Former name and Development of Yahashira Shrine == === Former name === The shrine was previously known as '''"Hachioji Daigongen"'''{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=872}}. The renaming was influenced by the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) decree issued by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} in {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}. This decree led to shrine name changes within the {{ill|Okazaki Domain|en|Okazaki Domain|WD=Q905686|lt=Okazaki Domain|1=Okazaki Domain}}, such as changing "Hachioji" to "Yahashira Shrine" and removing "Daigongen," a term heavily influenced by the Buddhist concept of [[honji suijaku]]{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=1460, 1461}}. === Development === The shrine originated from two shrines in the former Kake Village: one enshrining Ōhirume no Mikoto (Amaterasu Ōmikami) and Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi no Mikoto) in Hiromi, and another (Kumano) Gongen Shrine brought to Ishigasaki by Suzuki Bokuemon Shigetatsu, related to {{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu|1=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}, a [[daimyo]] of {{ill|Okazaki Castle|en|Okazaki Castle|WD=Q1013445|lt=Okazaki Castle|1=Okazaki Castle}}. A third shrine, Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine, was dedicated to the spirit of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama|1=Lady Tsukiyama}}{{SFn|『参河総視禄　欠村記』}}. Collectively, these shrines were called "Hachioji Daigongen." == Cultural Significance == The area, including the shrine, was highlighted as a scenic spot in ''Sanagawa no Meisho Zue,'' compiled by Natsume Kakei, a hardware merchant from {{ill|Toyohashi|en|Toyohashi|WD=Q336431|lt=Toyohashi|1=Toyohashi}}, over eight years starting in 1851{{SFn|『参河國名所圖繪　下巻』|p=409, 410}}. It is also noted on the ''Tōkaidō Bunken Nobiezu'' (1806) as the "Residence of Suzuki Bokuemon," near the shrine’s current location. == Recent Developments == According to ''Okazaki City History, Vol. 7,'' the Gongen Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were temporarily relocated to Hiromi in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 2 (1913), later consolidated into the current Yahashira Shrine site{{SFn|『岡崎市史　第七巻』|p=151}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Kumano Gongen]] (Five male and three female deities): ** [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] ** [[Ame-no-Hohi]] ** [[Amatsuhikone]] ** {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|WD=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} ** [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano Kusubi]] ** [[Tagorihime|Takirihime]] ** [[Ichikishima-hime]] ** [[Tagitsuhime]] * [[Amaterasu|Ōhirume no Mikoto]] (Amaterasu) * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] (Ōkuninushi) * Wakamiya Daigongen ({{ill|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|en|Matsudaira Nobuyasu|WD=Q1188704|lt=Matsudaira Nobuyasu|1=Matsudaira Nobuyasu}}) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Hime no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} (deity of fertility) * Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama|1=Lady Tsukiyama}}) == Festivals == * [[Reisai|Reitaisai]] (festival) – October 18 * [[Saitansai]] (New Year's Festival) – January 2 * {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Ritual) – 18th of every month (except September, held on the 10th as the [[Nihyakuhatsuka Matsuri]]) == Historical Milestones == * [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 7 (1579): Shrine founded{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　総集編20』|p=700}} * {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō|1=Shōhō}} 3 (1646): Reburial of {{ill|Lady Tsukiyama|en|Lady Tsukiyama|WD=Q4415657|lt=Lady Tsukiyama|1=Lady Tsukiyama}}’s remains{{SFn|『新編岡崎市史　近世3』|p=193}} * {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with Yahashira Shrine * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9 (1934): Relocation to its current location * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 52 (1977): Reconstruction of Lady Tsukiyama’s Kofun == Filming location == Due to [[:en:Okazaki City|Okazaki City]]’s efforts to attract film productions[https://fc.okazaki-kanko.jp/news?category_m Okazaki Film Commission], Yahashira Shrine has been featured as a filming location. === Featured Work === - Film: ''Kuchinai Sakura'' (''The Eternal Cherry Tree'')[https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee – Released June 21, 2024 == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/point/493 Yahashira Shrine] - Okazaki Official Tourism Website * [https://culture-pub.jp/kuchinaisakura_movie ''The Eternal Cherry Tree''] - Film Production Committee {{Shinto 横}} {{translated page|ja|八柱神社 (岡崎市)|version=104574714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Yahashira-jinja (Okazaki)}} [[ja:八柱神社 (岡崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q119324965}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 4 (1767): Merger with the local agricultural guardian shrine, Okuwa Shrine * [[Meiji]] 5 (1872): Registered as a [[Village Shrine]] * [[Meiji]] 42 (1909): Designated as a [[Shinpan Heihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine]] * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 2 (1913): Consolidation of Tsukiyama Shinmei Shrine with
